name: Abacha Ncha Salad
intro: Also known as African Salad, this Igbo delicacy is often eaten as a side dish or a snack. Abacha is prepared with fermented cassava and is paired with ugba (shredded oil bean seed) in a palm-oil based sauce. Dried abacha and ugba can be bought from well-stocked African grocery stores and will need to be soaked in boiling hot water to rehydrate them before adding to the salad. Garden eggs are an optional garnish to the salad but can be left out if you are struggling to find them or if they are not in season.
method: Combine the palm oil and baking soda mixture in a large bowl and mix well until emulsified and the oil turns paler. Add the meat alternative, along with the ground ehuru, cameroon pepper and salt to taste. Stir in the (rehydrated) ugba and abacha, making sure they are well coated with the sauce.
